verses.2: “Wait for me a little, and I will show you That there is still more to be said on God’s behalf.
verses.3: “I will bring my knowledge from afar, And ascribe righteousness to my Maker.
verses.4: “For truly my words are not false; One who is perfect in knowledge is with you.
verses.5: “Behold, God is mighty but does not reject anyone; He is mighty in strength of understanding.
verses.6: “He does not keep the wicked alive, But gives justice to the afflicted.
verses.7: “He does not withdraw His eyes from the righteous, But with kings on the throne He has seated them forever, and they are exalted.
verses.8: “And if they are bound in shackles, And are caught in the snares of misery,
verses.9: Then He declares to them their work And their wrongdoings, that they have been arrogant.
verses.10: “He opens their ears to instruction, And commands that they return from injustice.
verses.11: “If they listen and serve Him, They will end their days in prosperity, And their years in happiness.
verses.12: “But if they do not listen, they will perish by the sword, And die without knowledge.
verses.13: “But the godless in heart nurture anger; They do not call for help when He binds them.
verses.14: “They die in youth, And their life perishes among the cult prostitutes.
verses.15: “He rescues the afflicted in their misery, And opens their ears in time of oppression.
verses.16: “Then indeed, He induced you away from the mouth of distress, And instead of it, a broad place with no constraint; And your table was full of rich food.
verses.17: “But you were full of judgment on the wicked; Judgment and justice take hold of you.
verses.18: “ Beware that wrath does not entice you to mockery; And do not let the greatness of the ransom turn you aside.
verses.19: “Will your cry for help keep you from distress, Or all the exertions of your strength?
verses.20: “Do not long for the night, When people vanish in their places.
verses.21: “Be careful, do not turn to evil, For you preferred this to misery.
verses.22: “Behold, God is exalted in His power; Who is a teacher like Him?
verses.23: “Who has appointed Him His way, And who has said, ‘You have done wrong’?
verses.24: “Remember that you are to exalt His work, Of which people have sung.
verses.25: “All people have seen it; Mankind looks at it from afar.
verses.26: “Behold, God is exalted, and we do not know Him; The number of His years is unsearchable.
verses.27: “For He draws up the drops of water; They distill rain from its celestial stream,
verses.28: Which clouds pour down; They drip upon mankind abundantly.
verses.29: “Can anyone understand the spreading of the clouds, The thundering of His pavilion?
verses.30: “Behold, He spreads His lightning about Him, And He covers the depths of the sea.
verses.31: “For by them He judges peoples; He gives food in abundance.
verses.32: “He covers His hands with the lightning, And commands it to strike the target.
verses.33: “Its thundering voice declares His presence; The livestock also, concerning what is coming up.
